description: Docker save produces incomplete image archives with missing layer blobs because containerd's unpacker skips fetching layer content when a snapshot already exists, and moby unconditionally enables WithPullUnpack for all snapshotters

root_cause.summary: When pulling an image with WithPullUnpack, containerd's unpacker checks if a snapshot already exists for each layer (via sn.Stat on the chainID). If the snapshot exists, the unpacker returns nil immediately without ever initiating the layer content fetch. This means the compressed layer blob is never downloaded to the content store. On the moby side, daemon/containerd/image_pull.go unconditionally passes containerd.WithPullUnpack during pullTag, triggering this codepath for all pulls regardless of snapshotter type.
root_cause.mechanism: The unpacker's doUnpackFn checks sn.Stat(ctx, chainID) at the start. If the snapshot exists (err == nil), it returns nil — skipping both the snapshot preparation AND the content fetch goroutine (which is only started after sn.Prepare). The layer blob is never written to the content store. When docker save later tries to export the image, the missing blobs cause incomplete archives.

error_chain.action: Receives pull request, unconditionally appends containerd.WithPullUnpack to pull options (line 196)
Pull method sees pullCtx.Unpack=true, creates an Unpacker, attaches it to the image handler chain

BUG: For each layer, checks sn.Stat(ctx, chainID). If snapshot already exists (shared layer from another image), returns nil immediately. The content fetch goroutine is never started because it is only initialized after sn.Prepare succeeds.
Even if sn.Stat misses, sn.Prepare can return AlreadyExists. In that case, if sn.Stat(chainID) then succeeds, it also returns nil without fetching content.
Layer blob is never written — content store has no record of the layer's compressed data
Uses archive.WithSkipMissing to silently skip blobs not in the content store, producing an incomplete archive
Fails with missing layer error because the archive is incomplete

affected_services: containerd snapshotter (has snapshots but content not synced)
remediation: In moby daemon/containerd/image_pull.go: do not use WithPullUnpack for local snapshotters like overlayfs. Only use it for remote snapshotters where deferred layer download is needed. This is the approach taken by the fix PR (moby#49786). Alternatively, fix containerd's unpacker to always fetch layer content into the content store even when snapshots already exist, decoupling content availability from snapshot existence.
